title: The Dad Who Knew Too Little

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer presumably crashes car during opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated with character but routine by this point in series

jokes.text: It should be in a museum. But museums don't want it.
jokes.setup: Krusty suggesting his recliner belongs in a museum
jokes.punchline: Museums rejecting it completely undermines the prestige
jokes.explanation: Sharp subversion of expectation with excellent economy - perfectly captures celebrity delusion

jokes.text: I don't swim, so that's where I throw my shrimp shells.
jokes.punchline: Using luxury pool as garbage disposal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Krusty's gross-out celebrity lifestyle - specific and character-driven

jokes.text: Your monkey bit my kid again!
jokes.punchline: The mundane complaint involves a monkey attack
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist escalation of neighbor disputes, though not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Say hello to Bob- [Grunts] and Carol and Ted and Alice- [Grunting]
jokes.setup: Krusty introducing his monkeys with human names
jokes.punchline: The monkeys are apparently attacking him as he speaks
jokes.explanation: Nice visual comedy setup with movie reference (Bob & Carol & Ted & Alice) while being physically assaulted

jokes.text: That's me! You would like me. Loser!
jokes.setup: Kenny excited that diary mentions him
jokes.punchline: Immediately calling the person who likes him a loser
jokes.explanation: Captures teenage boy social awkwardness well but fairly predictable

jokes.text: [Book: Male Voice] Unauthorized user! Access denied! [Electricity Crackling] [Shrieking]
jokes.punchline: Electrocuting unauthorized users
jokes.explanation: Good tech absurdism but relies on shock value over wit

jokes.text: Turbo Diary, I love that I bought you.
jokes.setup: Girl expressing love for her diary
jokes.punchline: Awkward phrasing emphasizes the commercial transaction
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of commercial dialogue - highlights artificial nature of ads

jokes.text: Girltech is a division of Boytech.
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually controlled by male-oriented company
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary with perfect economy - captures corporate gender marketing cynicism

jokes.text: Lisa, nobody likes a shill.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking for the advertised toy
jokes.punchline: Homer calling his daughter a shill for wanting what she saw on TV
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Homer using sophisticated term while being manipulated by same commercial

jokes.text: [Chuckling] I love that little shill.
jokes.setup: Homer previously calling Lisa a shill negatively
jokes.punchline: Now finding her directness endearing
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Homer's inconsistent parenting - term of disapproval becomes endearment

jokes.text: A girl should have her own private diary. I had to share mine with my uncle.
jokes.setup: Marge supporting Lisa's need for privacy
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing disturbing childhood detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - disturbing implication delivered with Marge's innocent tone creates perfect contrast

jokes.text: Dad, you can't eat all those free samples. We've gotta get Lisa's present.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to keep Homer on task
jokes.punchline: Homer's presumably gorging himself on free samples
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer gluttony setup - predictable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Dad, you ate all the free samples. Now you're eating men's slacks.
jokes.punchline: Has moved on to eating clothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Homer's eating habits to absurd degree - specific detail makes it funnier

jokes.text: Heh. It's still better than Indian food.
jokes.punchline: Still preferring it to ethnic cuisine
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food prejudice - character-consistent but borders on cheap ethnic humor

jokes.text: Trying to keep those crushes secret, eh, Romeo?
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy assuming Homer has romantic secrets
jokes.explanation: Standard Comic Book Guy snark - character-appropriate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: It's not for me. I'm not a girl like you.
jokes.punchline: Homer insulting Comic Book Guy's masculinity
jokes.explanation: Homer getting a rare comeback on Comic Book Guy - satisfying but not exceptional

jokes.text: Finally, Smithers, a device worthy of storing my top secret enemies list.
jokes.setup: Burns excited about the high-tech diary
jokes.punchline: Wants to use children's toy for villainous purposes
jokes.explanation: Good Burns characterization - treating children's diary as serious evil tool

jokes.text: I'm ready, sir. Number one, the Kingston Trio.
jokes.punchline: First enemy is harmless 1960s folk group
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns absurdity - specific, unexpected choice that fits his ancient, petty character

jokes.text: [Shudders] Number two-
jokes.punchline: Physical revulsion at the memory
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Burns having visceral reaction to folk singers is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: [Book: Male Voice] Unauthorized user! Access denied! [Electricity Crackling] [Shrieking]
jokes.setup: Burns trying to use the diary
jokes.punchline: Gets electrocuted like in the commercial
jokes.explanation: Predictable callback but works for the plot - Burns getting comeuppance

jokes.text: Sir, I can offer you this Tickle Me Krusty, the most popular toy of 1999.
jokes.punchline: Obviously outdated reference to Tickle Me Elmo
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture parody - Comic Book Guy having old inventory fits character

jokes.text: Shop o' the mornin', Homer. Just call me 'Mall' Flanders.
jokes.setup: Flanders greeting Homer at the mall
jokes.punchline: Pun combining 'mall' with his name
jokes.explanation: Typical Flanders dad joke - groan-worthy pun fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: What are you buying? Jerk stuff?
jokes.setup: Homer asking what Flanders is shopping for
jokes.punchline: Assuming Flanders would buy 'jerk stuff'
jokes.explanation: Homer's simple antagonism toward Flanders - juvenile but character-consistent

jokes.text: Oh, you betcha. This kiosk sells personalized movies.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if he's buying jerk stuff
jokes.punchline: Flanders cheerfully agreeing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Flanders cheerfully accepting Homer's insult shows his nature

jokes.text: My hobbies include- [Ned's Voice] Being quiet during trips, clapping with songs, and diabetes.
jokes.punchline: Diabetes listed as a hobby alongside innocent activities
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - treating medical condition as hobby is perfectly unexpected

jokes.text: Hey, your kid is in the movie! And the movie knows his name!
jokes.punchline: Being amazed by basic technology premise
jokes.explanation: Homer's technological naivety - simple but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Maybe Lisa will like this better than the diary. Stupid Flanders, you're a genius.
jokes.punchline: Calling him stupid while acknowledging his intelligence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer contradiction - can't help insulting Flanders even while praising him

jokes.text: It's really cool. You can point a red dot at people's crotches from really far away.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining his laser pointer gift
jokes.punchline: Immediately focusing on inappropriate use
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart mischief - immediately sees prank potential in innocent gift

jokes.text: Hmm. There appears to be a red dot on my trouser front. I'd better lower them. [Sighs] The dot also appears to be on my underpants. Well, down they go.
jokes.setup: Ned noticing laser dot on his pants
jokes.punchline: Logically stripping to chase the dot
jokes.explanation: Good escalating physical comedy - Flanders' logical response makes it funnier

jokes.text: Hey, buddy. You better get that red dot checked out. My uncle died of crotch dot.
jokes.setup: Officer seeing Flanders with laser dot
jokes.punchline: Treating it as medical condition with family history
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist medical advice - 'crotch dot' as fatal condition is brilliantly specific

jokes.text: You were supposed to get her that stupid diary!
jokes.punchline: Marge's frustrated reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard domestic argument setup - functional but predictable

jokes.text: I don't like McNuggets. I'm a vegetarian. Still?
jokes.punchline: Homer surprised she's still vegetarian
jokes.explanation: Good character callback - Homer forgetting major aspect of Lisa's identity

jokes.text: Then you're not gonna like your other present.
jokes.punchline: Implying her other gift is also meat-related
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's continued cluelessness about Lisa - solid character moment

jokes.text: My favorite book is- [Homer's Voice] Magazines.
jokes.setup: Movie claiming to know Lisa's favorite book
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks magazines are books
jokes.explanation: Homer's ignorance about literature - treats magazines as sophisticated reading

jokes.text: Dad, that information is all wrong! Maggie's a baby, not my best friend. [Crying] You don't know anything about me.
jokes.setup: Lisa watching inaccurate movie about herself
jokes.punchline: Emotional breakdown over father's ignorance
jokes.explanation: Strong emotional comedy - Lisa's hurt at Homer's cluelessness drives the plot effectively

jokes.text: Maggie, you're her best friend. Go talk to her.
jokes.setup: Lisa upset that movie got her relationship with Maggie wrong
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately accepts movie version as truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - trusting his own bad information over reality

jokes.text: Good morning, honey. Did you cry out all your 'angries'?
jokes.setup: Lisa had emotional breakdown previous night
jokes.punchline: Homer treating complex emotions like a childish tantrum
jokes.explanation: Good Homer parenting cluelessness - reducing emotional complexity to simple terms

jokes.text: I'm still mad at you for chopping up my skateboard with an ax. I did it for the insurance money.
jokes.punchline: Homer casually admitting insurance fraud
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - Homer casually revealing criminal behavior as if reasonable

jokes.text: What's your favorite cigar size? Is it robusto? Is it?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to learn about Lisa
jokes.punchline: Asking eight-year-old about cigar preferences
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist parenting - Homer's questions completely age-inappropriate

jokes.text: But I'm full of questions. What's your favorite cigar size? Is it robusto? Is it?
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he has lots of questions about Lisa
jokes.punchline: Asking 8-year-old about cigar preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd question showing Homer's complete disconnect from child-appropriate interests

jokes.text: Yeah. At least you ain't aging six years for every one 'cause of your cow heart.
jokes.punchline: Barney one-upping with bizarre medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good Barney absurdism - specific weird medical detail fits his character

jokes.text: Ah. Well, whenever I gotta know something about a broad, I use this guy.
jokes.punchline: Barney referring to Lisa as 'a broad'
jokes.explanation: Barney's crude language about eight-year-old Lisa - character-appropriate but uncomfortable

jokes.text: He found out who was cobbling shoes for me at night. Turns out I have severe schizophrenia.
jokes.punchline: Detective solved mystery of Barney's own mental illness
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - treating mental illness discovery as detective work is brilliantly absurd

jokes.text: Maybe someday I'll turn into a swan. [Sighs] Oh, God.
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret and despair
jokes.explanation: Good Barney melancholy - absurd hope followed by crushing reality

jokes.text: 'Drunk Cop'- Is that you? No. Look lower.
jokes.setup: Homer looking for detective in directory
jokes.punchline: Detective presumably very short or in basement
jokes.explanation: Standard visual setup - implies detective is very short or operates from basement

jokes.text: It's always a dame. Usually with gams that don't quit... till they get to the shoes. And then they're only napping.
jokes.setup: Detective using noir clichés about women
jokes.punchline: Extending metaphor to absurd degree about legs 'napping' at shoes
jokes.explanation: Good noir parody - extends cliché to absurd logical conclusion

jokes.text: Chunky lover 53. It's one word. One word. Chunkylover53. At AOL. Dot com.
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining simple email format with embarrassing username
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's inappropriate email name and tech confusion

jokes.text: You know, you are the spitting image of the Aberdeen Strangler.
jokes.punchline: Casually comparing them to serial killer
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for sleazy detective - inappropriate comparison as intimidation

jokes.text: These are some nice looking papers on your desk. It would be a shame if somebody shuffled them. You wouldn't dare. Oh, wouldn't I? [Scoffs] I could easily put them back in their original order. Oh, really? No! [Echoing]
jokes.setup: Detective threatening to mess up papers
jokes.punchline: Official more terrified of having to reorganize than initial threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - bureaucrat's organizational terror trumps actual threat

jokes.text: You know, she's just an actress. Shut up! Some of us prefer illusion to despair.
jokes.setup: Detective commenting on Nelson's Snow White photo
jokes.punchline: Nelson's philosophical defense of fantasy
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Nelson showing unexpected depth and philosophy about escapism

jokes.text: Lisa Simpson is a girl at my school. I once picked my nose till it bled. [Sighs] About Lisa! Lisa Simpson is a girl at my school.
jokes.setup: Detective trying to get information about Lisa
jokes.punchline: Nelson repeating same useless information while oversharing
jokes.explanation: Standard Nelson dimwittedness - gross overshare mixed with unhelpful repetition

jokes.text: Lisa's pet peeve is phonies? I thought she loved them.
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding what 'pet peeve' means
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer vocabulary confusion - thinks pet peeve means favorite thing

jokes.text: What do we want? The gradual phaseout of animal testing over the next three years! When do we want it? Over the next three years!
jokes.punchline: Reasonable timeline undermines urgency of chant
jokes.explanation: Excellent political satire - realistic moderate demands undermine passionate protest format

jokes.text: So sad. Yet so sexy. Yet so delicious. [Slavers]
jokes.punchline: Moving from sympathy to attraction to appetite
jokes.explanation: Good Homer appetite escalation - can't maintain moral position when food involved

jokes.text: 'Report' is a daddy word that means 'loves his daughter.'
jokes.punchline: Homer making up terrible explanation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer panic lie - desperately trying to cover his deception

jokes.text: Early on, I was working under the theory that your daughter was a werewolf. Didn't pan out.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning werewolf investigation as professional service
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan absurdism - treating supernatural investigation as routine detective work

jokes.text: I can't believe a man who agreed to follow my daughter around, for money, would turn out to be a dirtbag!
jokes.setup: Homer hired detective for suspicious activity
jokes.punchline: Shocked that sketchy arrangement produced sketchy person
jokes.explanation: Good ironic realization - Homer's complete lack of awareness about obvious red flags

jokes.text: You'll regret the day you went to the expensive coffee place!
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic counter-threat about coffee expenses
jokes.explanation: Good Homer weakness - can only threaten about minor financial inconveniences

jokes.text: Cut it out, Lou. Uh, he's making funny faces. Sorry, Chief. One of these monkeys has the same name as my ex-wife.
jokes.setup: Chief trying to give serious statement
jokes.punchline: Officer distracted by monkey named after his ex-wife
jokes.explanation: Standard police incompetence - personal issues interfering with professional duty

jokes.text: Well, apparently there's a secret garden in all of us. And that Lisa Simpson is guilty!
jokes.setup: Chief examining evidence including book report
jokes.punchline: Philosophical observation followed by immediate wrong conclusion
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum - deep literary insight immediately followed by illogical conclusion

jokes.text: Would an innocent person flee? No, really. Tell me. I honestly don't know.
jokes.punchline: Actually asking for clarification on basic police logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent Wiggum moment - complete ignorance of basic police procedure

jokes.text: Names, please? Lady Penelope Ariel Ponyweather. Uh, Rock Strongo. Your real name. Uh, Lance Uppercut.
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasingly ridiculous action hero aliases
jokes.explanation: Good escalating alias comedy - Homer's terrible fake names get progressively more ridiculous

jokes.text: If the election were held today, would you vote for 'A,' the cops are there, or 'B,' you're free to talk?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to warn Marge covertly
jokes.punchline: Obvious coded message disguised as poll
jokes.explanation: Good Homer attempted cleverness - terrible at being subtle or covert

jokes.text: It's full of cigarette butts. So is this one. They must've come from over there.
jokes.setup: Homer and Lisa searching trash for food
jokes.punchline: Following cigarette trail to smoking monkeys
jokes.explanation: Functional plot advancement with mild humor - sets up discovery of test animals

jokes.text: You're not telling nobody nothing. Make one move and you'll get a belly full of the Flying Giuseppe.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous name for knife/weapon
jokes.explanation: Standard tough guy parody - giving weapon ridiculous ethnic name

jokes.text: Oh, Lisa has excellent hearing. Once when she was three, I was quietly sobbing in the closet, and she found me.
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing past emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Homer casually revealing traumatic parenting moment as practical information

jokes.text: Oh, Dad, you do remember something about me.
jokes.punchline: Lisa finding hope in disturbing anecdote
jokes.explanation: Good ironic sweetness - Lisa grateful for any personal memory, even traumatic one

jokes.text: How ironic. Now he's blind, after a life of enjoying being able to see.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's overly literal definition of irony
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor - Lisa's intellectual pretension creates non-ironic 'irony' observation

jokes.text: Cletus, if I find pig lipstick on your collar again, I'm not gonna let you sleep in the sty no more.
jokes.setup: Released test pigs in the wild
jokes.punchline: Hillbilly discovering pig with lipstick, assuming infidelity
jokes.explanation: Good callback resolution - cosmetic-wearing test pigs causing marital problems for hillbillies

jokes.text: [Book: Male Voice] Unauthorized user! Access denied! [Electricity Crackling] [Homer Shouting]
jokes.setup: Homer trying to read Bart a bedtime story
jokes.punchline: Gets electrocuted by the diary like Burns did earlier
jokes.explanation: Standard callback ending - Homer gets same comeuppance as Burns, predictable but satisfying

tmdb_overview: Homer hires a private detective to learn more about Lisa. But when Homer refuses to pay the detective's expenses, he frames Lisa for a crime she didn't commit, forcing her and Homer to go on the run.
